text.title: Batch + Cache Stack Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every batch+cache pipeline enforces.

rule.statement: The pipeline submits requests through the provider's batch endpoint (Anthropic Message Batches / OpenAI Batch API), not the synchronous endpoint.

rationale.source: Anthropic Message Batches docs; OpenAI Batch API docs

rule.rationale: 50% discount applies only on batch submissions; synchronous is full price.

rule.statement: The system prompt + tool definitions + canonical instructions are byte-identical across every batch item. Tested by a hash check before submission.
rule.rationale: Cache lookup requires exact byte match; a single space difference invalidates the cache.

rule.statement: cache_control marker is placed at the end of the longest stable prefix; everything before it is cached, everything after is item-specific.
rule.rationale: The marker tells the provider where the cacheable suffix ends; misplaced markers fail to hit cache.

rule.statement: Pipeline emits cache_read_input_tokens and cache_creation_input_tokens metrics per batch run. Target cache_hit_rate ≥ 0.9 once warm.
rule.rationale: Without monitoring, silent regressions (prompt edit, model version bump) drop hit rate without alerting.
